name: Steam Trap Energy Loss Calculator
description: Calculates the energy and cost losses from failed steam traps based on orifice size, steam pressure, and operating hours, following ISO 50001 and Six Sigma DMAIC principles.

inputs.label: Orifice Diameter
inputs.businessContext: Diameter of the trap orifice; typical failed traps have orifices from 1 mm to 20 mm.

businessContext_i18n.en: Diameter of the trap orifice; typical failed traps have orifices from 1 mm to 20 mm.

inputs.label: Steam Pressure (gauge)
inputs.businessContext: Gauge pressure of steam upstream of the trap. Common industrial range 3–15 bar_g.

businessContext_i18n.en: Gauge pressure of steam upstream of the trap. Common industrial range 3–15 bar_g.

inputs.label: Operating Hours per Year
inputs.businessContext: Annual operating hours of the steam system. Typical continuous process: 8000 h/yr.

businessContext_i18n.en: Annual operating hours of the steam system. Typical continuous process: 8000 h/yr.

inputs.label: Steam Cost
inputs.businessContext: Cost to generate one metric tonne of steam, including fuel, water treatment, and maintenance.

businessContext_i18n.en: Cost to generate one metric tonne of steam, including fuel, water treatment, and maintenance.

inputs.label: Trap Type
inputs.businessContext: Type of steam trap influences discharge coefficient and failure mode.

businessContext_i18n.en: Type of steam trap influences discharge coefficient and failure mode.

inputs.label: Failure Mode
inputs.businessContext: Failure mode determines effective orifice area and discharge coefficient.

businessContext_i18n.en: Failure mode determines effective orifice area and discharge coefficient.

inputs.label: Condensate Recovery
inputs.businessContext: Whether condensate is recovered; affects net energy loss calculation.

businessContext_i18n.en: Whether condensate is recovered; affects net energy loss calculation.

rules.message: Orifice diameter is unusually large for the given steam pressure. Check input values.
High operating hours with very low steam cost may indicate unrealistic assumptions.

thresholds.message: Annual energy loss exceeds 500,000 kWh. Immediate trap replacement recommended.
Annual cost loss exceeds $100,000. Perform root cause analysis (Six Sigma DMAIC).

formulas.label: Effective Orifice Area
formulas.description: Cross-sectional area of the orifice based on diameter and failure mode factor.

formulas.label: Steam Flow Rate
formulas.description: Mass flow rate of steam through the failed trap using Napier's formula for saturated steam.

formulas.label: Annual Steam Loss (Mass)
formulas.description: Total mass of steam lost per year.

formulas.label: Energy Loss (kWh)
formulas.description: Thermal energy lost in the steam, using enthalpy of vaporization at given pressure.

variables.h_fg: enthalpy of vaporization at steam_pressure (kJ/kg) – interpolated from steam tables

formulas.label: Condensate Recovery Adjustment
formulas.description: If condensate is recovered, 20% of energy is recovered; otherwise 0%.

formulas.label: Annual Cost Loss
formulas.description: Monetary value of lost steam energy.

formulas.label: CO2 Emissions
formulas.description: Indirect CO2 emissions from wasted steam (assuming natural gas boiler, 0.2 kg CO2/kWh).

primary.label: Total Annual Cost Loss
primary.description: Primary result: total monetary loss due to failed steam trap.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Orifice Diameter
hiddenLossDrivers.impact: Quadratic effect on area and flow

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Failure Mode
hiddenLossDrivers.impact: Factor of 1.0 vs 0.3 on effective area

suggestedActions.label: Conduct system-wide steam trap survey
suggestedActions.expectedSavings: Variable, typically 10-20% of total steam loss

suggestedActions.label: Install continuous monitoring on critical traps
suggestedActions.expectedSavings: Reduction in failure detection time by 80%

suggestedActions.label: Improve condensate recovery system
suggestedActions.expectedSavings: Up to 20% of energy loss recovered
